---
title: "UIAO Technical Specification Template"
author: "UIAO Modernization Program"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
format:
  html: default
  docx: default
---

# UIAO Technical Specification Template (Canonical)

> The UIAO-approved, deterministic specification template for Adapter Contracts, Governance Specs, and Layer Definitions.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Version** | 1.0 |
| **Date** | 2026-04-03 |
| **Status** | Active |
| **Governed by** | `data/document-skeleton.yml` (variant: `technical_specification`) |
| **Style authority** | `data/style-guide.yml`, `docs/STYLE-GUIDE.qmd` |
| **Document ID format** | `UIAO_[SYSTEM]_SPEC_[VERSION]` |

---

## Overview

This template is a specialization of the UIAO Canonical Document Skeleton for technical specifications. It adds Definitions, Contract Requirements (with RFC 2119 language), and Operational Requirements sections that do not appear in the base skeleton.

Use this template when creating:

- Adapter Contracts
- Governance Specifications
- Layer Definitions
- System Integration Specifications

---

## Template Structure

### TITLE: [System / Adapter / Layer Name] Technical Specification

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Document ID** | UIAO_[SYSTEM]_SPEC_[VERSION] |
| **Status** | Current |

---

### 1. Purpose

A concise statement of what this specification defines.

> Example: "This document defines the canonical contract for the Database Adapter."

---

### 2. Scope

- In-scope components
- Out-of-scope components
- Boundaries (cloud, security, operational)

---

### 3. Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| Term 1 | Definition |
| Term 2 | Definition |
| Term 3 | Definition |

---

### 4. Architecture Overview

#### 4.1 System Context

Insert diagram reference per UIAO diagram standards:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Diagram ID** | Diagram 4.1-A |
| **Dimensions** | 1600x900 |
| **Background** | White (#FFFFFF) |
| **Description** | MISSING (to be provided by author) |
| **Renderer** | PlantUML or Gemini AI |

#### 4.2 Components

- Component A
- Component B
- Component C

---

### 5. Contract Requirements

All requirements in this section MUST use RFC 2119 language: **MUST**, **SHOULD**, **MAY**.

#### 5.1 Inputs

Define all required inputs, data formats, and sources.

#### 5.2 Outputs

Define all expected outputs, data formats, and destinations.

#### 5.3 Error Conditions

Define all error states, failure modes, and recovery behavior.

#### 5.4 Logging and Telemetry

Define logging requirements, telemetry signals, and audit trail obligations.

#### 5.5 Security Controls

Define authentication, authorization, encryption, and compliance requirements. Map to NIST 800-53 Rev 5 controls where applicable.

---

### 6. Governance Integration

- FIMF alignment
- Risk controls
- KPIs
- Audit trail requirements

---

### 7. Operational Requirements

#### 7.1 Performance

Define latency, throughput, and capacity requirements.

#### 7.2 Availability

Define uptime targets, failover behavior, and recovery time objectives.

#### 7.3 Determinism

Define how this component satisfies the UIAO determinism principle: given the same inputs, the system MUST produce the same outputs.

#### 7.4 Drift Controls

Define how configuration drift is detected, reported, and remediated.

#### 7.5 Deployment Boundaries

Define where this component runs (Commercial Cloud, GCC-Moderate, on-premises) and any boundary constraints.

---

### 8. Appendices

| Appendix | Title |
|---|---|
| A | Image Descriptions |
| B | Copy Section |
| C | Change Log (no references to prior versions) |

---

## Usage Notes

- Every field marked MISSING must be filled before the document is considered canonical
- The Document ID format `UIAO_[SYSTEM]_SPEC_[VERSION]` must be unique across the corpus
- This template inherits all rules from `data/style-guide.yml` and `docs/STYLE-GUIDE.qmd`
- Contract Requirements (Section 5) must use RFC 2119 language exclusively
- The Appendix structure (A-C) overrides the base skeleton's A-H order for this variant

---

## Related Files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `data/document-skeleton.yml` | Base skeleton with `technical_specification` variant |
| `data/style-guide.yml` | Machine-readable style governance |
| `docs/STYLE-GUIDE.qmd` | Human-readable style guide |
| `docs/DOCUMENT-SKELETON.qmd` | Base document skeleton reference |
